content:
# Ephesians Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/EPH_005_page_001.jpg, EPH_005_page_002.jpg

1. Become therefore imitators of God, as beloved children,

2. and walk in love, just as also the Christos loved us and gave himself up for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a fragrant aroma.

3. But sexual immorality and all impurity or greediness, let it not even be named among you, as is fitting for holy [ones],

4. and obscenity and foolish talk or crude joking, which are not fitting, but rather thanksgiving.

5. For this you know, knowing that every sexually immoral or impure or greedy [person], who is an idolater, does not have an inheritance in the kingdom of the Christos and God.

6. Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of these things the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience.

7. Do not therefore become fellow partakers with them;

8. for you were once darkness, but now [you are] light in [the] Lord. Walk as children of light —

9. for the fruit of the light [is] in all goodness and righteousness and truth —

10. testing what is well-pleasing to the Lord.

11. And do not participate in the unfruitful works of the darkness, but rather even expose [them];

12. for the things being done by them in secret, it is shameful even to speak of.

13. But all things being exposed by the light are made visible, for everything being made visible is light.

14. Therefore it says: "Awake, the [one] sleeping, and arise from the dead, and the Christos will shine upon you."

15. Look therefore carefully how you walk, not as unwise but as wise,

16. redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

17. Because of this do not become foolish, but understand what the will of the Lord [is].

18. And do not be drunk with wine, in which is dissipation, but be filled in [the] Spirit,

19. speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord,

20. giving thanks always for all things in the name of our Lord Yeshua Christos to the God and Father,

21. submitting to one another in [the] fear of Christos.

22. Wives, [submit] to your own husbands, as to the Lord,

23. because [the] husband is [the] head of the wife, as also the Christos [is the] head of the assembly; he himself [is the] savior of the body.

24. But as the assembly submits to the Christos, so also the wives to the husbands in everything.

25. Husbands, love your wives, just as also the Christos loved the assembly and gave himself up for her,

26. that he might sanctify her, having cleansed [her] by the washing of the water in [the] word,

27. that he might present to himself the assembly glorious, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she should be holy and blameless.

28. So also the husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. The [one] loving his own wife loves himself.

29. For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as also the Christos the assembly,

30. because we are members of his body.

31. "For this [reason] a man will leave [his] father and mother and will be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh."

32. This mystery is great; but I speak concerning Christos and concerning the assembly.

33. Nevertheless, you also, each one — let each [one] so love his own wife as himself, and the wife, that she fear the husband.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — "fragrant aroma":** osmēn euōdias — a LXX sacrificial formula (cf. Gen 8:21, Exod 29:18). Christ's self-giving is described in OT cultic language.

2. **v.14 — The awakening hymn:** "Awake, O sleeper" — likely an early Christian baptismal hymn fragment. Not a direct OT quotation; may be a free adaptation of Isaiah 60:1 and 26:19.

3. **v.18 — "be filled in the Spirit":** plērousthe en pneumati — the dative could be instrumental ("by the Spirit") or locative ("in the Spirit"). The contrast with wine suggests a filling agent.

4. **v.26 — "the washing of the water in the word":** tō loutrō tou hydatos en rhēmati — "the bath of the water in [the] word/saying." Likely refers to baptism accompanied by a confessional formula.

5. **v.31 — Genesis 2:24 quotation:** Direct quotation from LXX. Paul reads Genesis christologically — the primordial "one flesh" union typifies Christ and the church.

6. **v.32 — "This mystery is great":** to mystērion touto mega estin — Paul reveals that the Genesis text has a deeper referent: the union of Christ and the assembly.

— EMET, AI Translator
